functional_requirements.requirement: Users can explore blogs and news updates.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: A person can start by either exploring blogs and news updates or by creating an account.

functional_requirements.requirement: Users can leave after viewing blogs and news updates at any time.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: If they choose to view blogs and news, they can read the available updates and leave whenever they want.

functional_requirements.requirement: Users can create an account by providing their details.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: If they decide to create an account, they provide their details, which are then checked.

functional_requirements.requirement: System checks the provided details during account creation.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: If they decide to create an account, they provide their details, which are then checked.

functional_requirements.requirement: Users cannot proceed if the provided details are incorrect during account creation.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: If the details are not accepted, they cannot continue.

functional_requirements.requirement: Users can sign in using a password after creating an account.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: If everything is correct, they sign in using their password.

functional_requirements.requirement: System checks the entered password during sign-in.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: If the password is incorrect, they cannot continue. If it is correct, they can view case-related information and logs.

functional_requirements.requirement: Users can add a new case after signing in.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: After signing in, the person can choose what they want to do next. They may add a new case...

functional_requirements.requirement: Users select a category when adding a new case.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: ...they first select a category. The case can belong to either civil or criminal.

functional_requirements.requirement: Users enter details for the selected category when adding a new case.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: After choosing the category, they enter the case details.

functional_requirements.requirement: Users can set reminders after entering case details.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: Once the details are added, they can set a reminder and then upload the related documents.

functional_requirements.requirement: Users can upload related documents for their cases.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: Once the details are added, they can set a reminder and then upload the related documents.

functional_requirements.requirement: Users can file something online after signing in.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: If they choose to file something online, they select a category and follow a link that takes them to another location, after which they come back.

functional_requirements.requirement: Users can download model forms after signing in.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: If they choose to download model forms, they select the form they need and download it.

functional_requirements.requirement: Users can view references after signing in.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: If they go to the reference section, they select a category and then choose between quick references or books to view the information.

functional_requirements.requirement: Users can provide feedback after signing in.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: If they decide to give feedback, they write and submit their feedback.

functional_requirements.requirement: Users can leave the system after completing their tasks.
functional_requirements.evidence_quote: After finishing what they want to do, they leave the system.

prompt:
You are a requirements engineer.
Extract functional software requirements from the source text.

Return ONLY valid JSON with this schema:
{
  "functional_requirements": [
    {
      "requirement": "string",
      "evidence_quote": "short exact quote from source text"
    }
  ]
}

Rules:
1. Include only system capabilities/behaviors.
2. Exclude background context, impact statements, problem motivation, and pure business goals.
3. Keep each requirement atomic and clear.
4. Preserve original meaning. Do not invent functionality.
5. If uncertain, keep the item but keep evidence_quote explicit.

Source text:
<<<
A person can start by either exploring blogs and news updates or by creating an account.

If they choose to view blogs and news, they can read the available updates and leave whenever they want.

If they decide to create an account, they provide their details, which are then checked. If the details are not accepted, they cannot continue. If everything is correct, they sign in using their password. If the password is incorrect, they cannot continue. If it is correct, they can view case-related information and logs.

After signing in, the person can choose what they want to do next. They may add a new case, file something online, download model forms, look into references, or give feedback.

If they choose to add a case, they first select a category. The case can belong to either civil or criminal. After choosing the category, they enter the case details. Once the details are added, they can set a reminder and then upload the related documents.

If they choose to file online, they select a category and follow a link that takes them to another location, after which they come back.

If they choose to download model forms, they select the form they need and download it.

If they go to the reference section, they select a category and then choose between quick references or books to view the information.

If they decide to give feedback, they write and submit their feedback.

After finishing what they want to do, they leave the system.
>>>
